data.content_ref: inline:The account claims a pandemic selectively devastated densely populated palatial centers and their administrative classes, leaving a power vacuum. Yet the scope excludes Egypt, which possessed the most densely populated urban and palatial-administrative centers in the Eastern Mediterranean (e.g., Memphis, Pi-Ramesses) and was deeply integrated into the same trade networks. The account offers no mechanism for why a pathogen traveling via these trade routes would bypass Egypt's equally dense and interconnected administrative centers while devastating all others, making the selective survival claim inconsistent with the proposed epidemiological mechanism.
